# Figure description: скв_392.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Колонки_ скважин/скв_392.jpg`

---

## Figure Type

This is a **borehole/well log (геологический разрез скважины)** — a detailed lithological and geophysical well log presented in a long vertical strip format, typical of Soviet-era drilling documentation. It appears to combine a **lithological column** with at least one **geophysical curve** (likely a self-potential or resistivity log, or possibly a gamma-ray log), along with tabular data columns on both sides. The format is consistent with standard Soviet бурение (drilling) log documentation used in regional geological surveys.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers a **single borehole (скважина)**, the identity of which is partially visible in the header — the title text at the top appears to read **"УФИ КОПИТОМОВСКИЙ"** or similar (difficult to resolve at this resolution), suggesting a named survey locality in northern Kazakhstan. The log appears to cover a substantial depth interval, potentially several hundred metres, based on the length of the strip. The header references what appears to be a **геологический разрез** (geological cross-section/log) with associated metadata including survey organization, date, and possibly map sheet coordinates consistent with sheets **M-42 or N-42**.

---

## Key Content

The log is structured as a multi-column vertical strip with the following apparent components:

- **Left side columns**: Tabular data including depth markers (глубина), likely sample numbers or core recovery intervals, lithological descriptions, and possibly stratigraphic age assignments. Several horizontal ruled divisions suggest distinct lithological or stratigraphic units.

- **Central geophysical curve**: A continuous, irregular waveform trace runs vertically through the centre of the log. The curve shows **high-frequency variability** throughout, with several notable zones of increased amplitude fluctuation — particularly in the upper third and lower quarter of the log. This is consistent with a **КС (кажущееся сопротивление / apparent resistivity)** or **ПС (потенциал самополяризации / self-potential)** curve, or possibly a **гамма-каротаж (gamma-ray log)**. The irregular, spiky character throughout suggests lithological heterogeneity or alternating resistive and conductive beds.

- **Right side columns**: Additional tabular data, likely including formation names, rock descriptions, or geophysical parameter values corresponding to depth intervals.

- **Horizontal ruled divisions**: Multiple prominent horizontal lines divide the log into stratigraphic or depth units, with some appearing more heavily marked — possibly indicating formation boundaries or casing depths.

- **Lower section annotation**: Near the bottom of the log, there is a text block that appears to read **"КОНТРОЛЬНЫЙ АНАЛИЗ"** (control analysis), suggesting that the lower portion of the borehole received additional analytical attention, possibly geochemical or mineralogical sampling.

- **Small inset box**: A small rectangular inset appears near the lower-middle portion of the log, possibly a legend or scale reference for the geophysical curve.

No colour coding is apparent — the document appears to be a **monochrome/pencil or ink original**, reproduced photographically.

## Research Value

This borehole log provides a continuous record of **subsurface lithology and geophysical response** at a specific drill site within the M-42/N-42 survey area, allowing correlation of rock types and formation boundaries with depth. The geophysical curve (likely resistivity or SP) would help identify **zones of anomalous conductivity or natural potential**, which in a mineral exploration context could indicate **sulphide mineralization, altered zones, or aquifer horizons** relevant to the broader regional assessment. Combined with the other boreholes and geophysical profiles in Report 36572, this log would contribute to building a **3D subsurface model** for evaluating mineral and structural targets across the survey area.
